name: Helpful Hints for Handling Phyllo Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. If possible, purchase phyllo that has not been frozen. It can be found in the refrigerator section of most Greek and some Middle Eastern delis.
2. Let the package come to room temperature before opening it. The sheets are easier to work with at room temperature.
3. Remove the sheets you are working with and spray or brush them with oil, butter, or nonstick cooking spray as quickly as possible. Keep the unused portions covered with a towel or plastic wrap.
4. Work quickly; do not expose reserved sheets to the air or they will dry out and disintegrate.
5. Sprinkle bread crumbs between the layers to keep them from sticking together when they bake. This is particularly helpful if you plan to refrigerate or freeze the recipe before baking.
